title: festuca marginata subsp. alopecuroides
intro: Festuca marginata subsp. alopecuroides, also known as Crested Fescue, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family, Poaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to its high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Crested Fescue is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
